Programme narrative · April 2026
A Resort Diverts its Linen —
Into the Hands of Communities Who Need It.
In April 2026, Mövenpick Resort Jimbaran Bali redirected its surplus linen inventory — 2,625 items across 14 categories — away from waste disposal and into the hands of five organisations serving vulnerable people across Bali. The programme was facilitated by Keeper and structured entirely as donation: no commercial sale took place.

Four registered organisations received the bulk of the inventory: Bali Children Project (955 items), PKP Women Safe House (1,010 items), Bali Life Foundation (525 items), and Bali Street Mums (102 items). A fifth, informal pathway — 33 items guided by the elders of the Banjar of Cemagi — directed linen to a single family living with nothing, following the traditional Balinese community governance structure of the banjar. This pathway, while small in volume, reflects the programme's most direct connection to individual human need.

The total weight diverted — estimated at 1,082 kg using WRAP standard textile benchmarks — represents a measurable environmental saving of approximately 532 kg CO₂e versus landfill disposal. Weights are Keeper-estimated from published unit benchmarks; they have not been independently verified by hotel weigh-in and should be treated as indicative for reporting purposes.

Community Pathway · Cemagi Banjar · 04 of 05
A Family Living with Nothing.
Guided by Village Elders.
33 items were allocated through the traditional governance structure of the Banjar of Cemagi. The banjar's elders identified a specific family in their community living with nothing, and directed the allocation accordingly. This pathway falls outside the formal NGO structure — it is documented here for transparency as an informal, community-led redirection, and represents the most direct expression of this programme's human intent.

No testimonial is solicited from a private family. The banjar elder's guidance serves as the authorisation for this pathway.
In-kind donation value · secondhand market

Linen In-Kind Donation Value — All Pathways

Tokopedia / Carousell ID / Pasar Loak · Apr 2026 · used/old condition · conservative lower-mid pricing
Methodology: Secondhand market prices observed on Tokopedia, Carousell Indonesia, and Pasar Loak (Bali/Jakarta), April 2026. All items assessed in "old/used" condition — conservative lower-mid of observed listings applied. Hotel-grade linen commands a slight premium over domestic quality in the used market due to fabric weight and construction, but this is not factored in (conservative treatment). Exchange rate IDR 17,700/USD applied for USD equivalents (Yahoo Finance / Investing.com, 25 May 2026). This valuation is for ESG/CSR reporting and tentative tax substantiation only — not formal tax advice.
Item TypeQtyIDR/pcTotal Value (IDR)Condition
Total in-kind donation value — Mövenpick Jimbaran April 2026 IDR 32,417,500 ≈ USD 1,831
All prices are indicative secondhand market estimates — not audited values. For formal tax filing, obtain sign-off from a registered Indonesian tax consultant (konsultan pajak terdaftar). Prices: bed sheets IDR 18,000–25,000; duvet covers IDR 22,000–28,000; pillow cases IDR 7,000–8,000; face towels IDR 3,500–4,000; hand towels IDR 8,000; bath sheets IDR 15,000; bath mats IDR 10,000; bathrobes IDR 28,000–35,000. Source: Tokopedia, Carousell ID, Pasar Loak Bali/Jakarta, Apr 2026.
In-kind donation value · linen & rag breakdown
Quality control · Linen condition assessment · April 2026
Of 2,625 items diverted: 1,969 donated as linen, 656 repurposed as rags.
Of the 2,625 items diverted from disposal, 1,969 items (75%) were donated as functional linen — usable bed sheets, towels, bath mats, and bathrobes, distributed across five community pathways. The remaining 656 items (25%) could not serve their original purpose due to rips, stains, and wear beyond functional use; these were repurposed as rags — cleaning cloths, workshop material, and practical household use. Landfill was avoided for every single item. The CO₂e figure and total diversion count are unaffected. In-kind donation value is reported only for the 1,969 items received and used as linen; rags carry no secondhand market value as finished goods and are recorded at IDR 0.

Tax compliance documentation

Indonesian Tax Documentation Trail

UU PPh No.36/2008 · PMK 90/PMK.03/2020 · Tentative — requires tax adviser sign-off
Under Indonesian tax law, in-kind donations to a registered yayasan may be treated as excluded from PPh income tax objects (Pasal 4 ayat 3, UU PPh No.36/2008) where conditions under PMK 90/PMK.03/2020 are met. Whether this treatment applies, and how to report it in the SPT Tahunan, depends on the hotel's tax position and must be confirmed by a registered Indonesian tax consultant (konsultan pajak terdaftar) before any filing. Note: the Cemagi Banjar pathway is an informal allocation and is not eligible for NGO-based tax treatment — only the 4 registered-organisation pathways may qualify. The documentation below supports ESG/CSR reporting and, where tax adviser sign-off is obtained, SPT substantiation.

The case for a structured system
Hotels generate meaningful social and environmental value through their surplus — but only if there is a system to capture it. Without structure, the carbon goes untracked, the storage stays full, the connections stay narrow, and the story is never told. With Keeper, a single decision activates an ecosystem.

Hidden Value Lost Without a Structured Diversion System

Impact of unstructured vs. Keeper-facilitated approach
The Keeper thesis
Keeper turns end-of-use assets from a write-off
into a value event — environmental, social,
financial, and reputational, simultaneously.
The right infrastructure makes value visible that was always there.
Mövenpick Resort Jimbaran chose differently. By working within the Keeper framework, a single decision — to redirect rather than dispose — activated five distinct pathways to need, cleared storage space, produced a fully documented ESG record, and generated IDR 24.3M adjusted in-kind donation value. All of it completed in under seven days.
Value Category Without a System With Keeper — This Programme Impact Captured
Carbon Tracking Not recorded. Weight untracked, no CO₂e calculation, no methodology. 532 kg CO₂e avoided. WRAP CarbonWARM2 textile factor disclosed. Green Globe–aligned methodology prepared for audit review. FULLY CAPTURED
NGO Connection Donation ad hoc or not at all. No verified NGO, no testimonial, no documentation. 5 verified pathways. PKP Women Safe House, Bali Children Project, Bali Street Mums, Bali Life Foundation, Cemagi Banjar. Testimonials received. Full documentation trail. VERIFIED & DOCUMENTED
In-Kind Value Donation value undocumented. PPh exclusion not claimed. No audit trail. IDR 24.3M in-kind donation value documented (1,969 linen items). 10-item tax documentation trail prepared. PPh exclusion applicable to 4 registered Yayasan pathways. IDR 24.3M DOCUMENTED
ESG Reporting No quantifiable data tracked and reported to GM, owner, investors or certifiers. Full CO₂e methodology. GHG Protocol Scope 3 landfill-avoided baseline. Green Globe Silver-level compliance. Press-ready impact narrative created. AUDIT-SUPPORTIVE
Staff & Brand Value Redirection invisible to staff and guests. Limited staff engagement opportunities. No story to tell internally or externally. Staff support to take action. Photo evidence from handover. Community testimonials. Press-ready impact report. Social media assets — the story of where your linen went, ready for Mövenpick's channels. STORY CREATED
Staff Time Fragmented across departments. No single owner. Repeated effort per disposal cycle. Knowledge sitting with individuals, not processes. Single point of coordination. Inventory, logistics, documentation and reporting centralised through Keeper. Hotel staff time protected. Knowledge lives in a process, not a person. CENTRALISED & EXPANDING
Good intention without infrastructure leaves value on the table — financial, environmental, and reputational. This programme demonstrates what structured circular stewardship captures in a single diversion cycle. The storeroom becomes a resource. The surplus becomes a programme. The disposal becomes a donation.
Methodology — Green Globe submission statement
Green Globe & GHG Protocol Methodology Disclosure

CO₂e Factors — WRAP CarbonWARM2 v0.3 (DEFRA-commissioned, MELMod landfill model): Material-specific landfill-avoidance factor applied uniformly: Textiles = 0.492 kg CO₂e/kg — applied across all 14 item types (bed sheets, duvet covers, pillow cases, face towels, hand towels, bath sheets, bath mats, bathrobes). This is the standard textile factor under WRAP CarbonWARM2 and is consistent with Silver-level Green Globe methodology. Source: wrap.ngo/resources/report/carbon-waste-and-resources-metric-carbonwarm2 · gov.uk/government/collections/government-conversion-factors-for-company-reporting.

CO₂e Methodology — Donation Programme: This programme is 100% donation with no commercial resale component. Accordingly, only the landfill-avoided baseline (Method A — WRAP CarbonWARM2) is applicable. The reuse-displacement methodology (GHG Protocol Scope 3 Category 5, Method C) — which was applied to the TV resale stream in the Pullman Legian programme — is not applicable here as there is no confirmed commercial buyer whose purchase displaces new production. The 532.18 kg CO₂e figure represents estimated landfill-avoided emissions only and is both methodologically appropriate and conservative.

Weight Sources & Verification: All unit weights are Keeper estimates derived from published hotel-grade GSM benchmarks (WRAP CarbonWARM2 standard textile weights, Hotemax, King of Cotton, The Linen Factory). Item counts are taken directly from the hotel-supplied distribution spreadsheet (Movenpick_Final_distribution.xlsx, April 2026). Weights have not been verified by physical hotel weigh-in. For formal Green Key or Green Globe audit submission, the hotel should provide actual weighed inventory records.

In-Kind Valuation: Secondhand market prices observed on Tokopedia, Carousell Indonesia, and Pasar Loak (Bali/Jakarta), April 2026. All items priced in "used/old" condition at conservative lower-mid of observed listings. Hotel-grade linen premium not applied (conservative treatment). This valuation is for ESG/CSR reporting and tax documentation substantiation only — not formal tax advice. Exchange rate IDR 17,700/USD applied (Yahoo Finance / Investing.com, 25 May 2026).

Carbon Equivalencies: Car: EPA avg petrol vehicle 0.251 kg CO₂/km (EPA GHG Equivalencies Calculator, 2024) → ~2,120 km. Trees: US Forest Service avg 0.02 MTCO₂/tree/yr (general mixed forest, all ages) → ~27 trees. Homes: PLN Indonesia grid 0.87 kg CO₂/kWh × avg Indonesian household 1,530 kWh/yr → ~0.40 homes. Flights: ICAO Carbon Calculator 2024, Bali–Jakarta economy return ~185 kg CO₂/pax → ~2.9 flights.

Green Globe Compliance: WRAP CarbonWARM2 textile landfill-avoided factor = Silver-level methodology compliance. Standard disclosures for audit: (1) UK WRAP/DEFRA factors applied as internationally recognised proxy — Indonesia-specific equivalent not published at equivalent granularity; (2) weights from WRAP benchmarks, not physical hotel weighing — physical weighing recommended for formal regulatory filing; (3) GHG Protocol Scope 3 framing — landfill-avoided baseline only, donation programme, no resale displacement credit claimed; (4) Cemagi Banjar pathway is informal — 33 items documented for transparency but excluded from any certified reporting framework; (5) item counts hotel-reported from distribution spreadsheet. This methodology statement is suitable for formal Green Globe submission with the caveats noted above.

Limitations & Assumptions

  • In-kind valuation — rag reclassification: 656 items (25% of inventory) were received by Yayasan organisations as rags — not usable as linen due to rips and stains — and are recorded at IDR 0 donation value. Secondhand market pricing (Tokopedia / Carousell ID / Pasar Loak, April 2026) applies only to the 1,969 items donated as functional linen. CO₂e and total diversion figures cover all 2,625 items including rags.
  • Estimated weights: All unit weights are derived from published benchmarks — not physical weighing of the specific items. For formal audit, hotel weigh-in is recommended.
  • Hotel-reported item counts: Distribution quantities are stated in the hotel-supplied spreadsheet and have not been independently verified by Keeper against physical stock.
  • Secondhand valuation: Market prices are indicative estimates from observed listings (Apr 2026) — not audited valuations. Declared in-kind value may differ from a formal independent appraisal.
  • Cemagi Banjar pathway: Informal allocation (33 items) not attributable to a registered yayasan. Excluded from NGO-based tax treatment and formal certified reporting unless supported by banjar letter or equivalent written confirmation.
  • Diversion claim scope: "100% of designated inventory redirected" refers to items designated for this programme — not a claim about the hotel's total waste stream or downstream end-of-life handling.
